title: crataegus oxyacantha var. triloba
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. triloba, also known as the three-lobed hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn tree, which is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of C. oxyacantha var. triloba have been studied for their potential to support cardiovascular health, with flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) being key bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant may include reduced blood pressure, improved lipid profiles, and enhanced cardiovascular function, which may help to alleviate symptoms of heart failure and angina. C. oxyacantha var. triloba can be prepared as herbal supplements, teas, and extracts, which are often used to promote cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart disease.
